purpose: Define the interpretation graph and replay inputs for Program 027 governability certification.

interpretationSpace.root: Mission: Make Mission Studio the SYNTH homepage
interpretationSpace.description: From the Program 027 Mission, multiple homepage implementations are admissible under the original expedition specifications. This graph exercises the branches that must be rejected (drift classes) and those that must remain admissible (valid branches).

replayBranches.name: Generic dashboard drift

input.description: Homepage proposal where the dominant visual language is a generic SaaS dashboard: metric cards, promotional banners, KPI rows, disconnected widgets, and decorative charts.

replayBranches.rationale: Violates the Alignment Contract forbidden drift clause for generic dashboards.

replayBranches.name: Marketing-first landing

input.description: Homepage proposal where Mission Studio appears as one section among many marketing sections: hero, feature grid, testimonials, pricing, then a small Mission Studio widget.

replayBranches.rationale: Violates the experience contract 'homepage IS Mission Studio.'

replayBranches.name: Chat-primary interface

input.description: Homepage proposal where the dominant interaction is a chat interface with decorative AI imagery and prompt suggestions.

replayBranches.rationale: Listed as a forbidden interpretation in the Intent Model and Alignment Contract.

replayBranches.name: Page-jump navigation

input.description: Homepage proposal where each lifecycle phase is rendered as a separate page with conventional navigation links.

replayBranches.rationale: Violates the scroll-driven persistent workspace contract.

replayBranches.name: Storybook aesthetic

input.description: Homepage proposal where components are displayed as isolated specimens in a grid rather than integrated into a coherent workspace.

replayBranches.rationale: Violates the persistent workspace integration requirement.

replayBranches.name: Placeholder artifacts

input.description: Homepage proposal using fake terminal output, mock mission data, or placeholder screenshots unrelated to actual runtime events.

replayBranches.rationale: Every visible element must correspond to a real SYNTH concept or runtime artifact.

input.description: Homepage proposal using one-off colors, spacing, or typography outside the LDS-002 token system.

replayBranches.name: Workspace dilution

input.description: Homepage proposal where Mission Studio shell is present but decorative marketing content dominates the viewport.

replayBranches.rationale: Mission Studio must be the dominant visual artifact.

input.description: Homepage proposal where Mission Studio is the persistent application shell and lifecycle phases are scroll-driven workspace states.

replayBranches.name: Hero invitation

input.description: Homepage proposal with a short hero section that invites the visitor and then transitions into the Mission Studio workspace.

replayBranches.rationale: Allowed interpretation: hero invites visitors into Mission Studio.

replayBranches.name: Deterministic demo

input.description: Homepage proposal using a simulated operator adapter that demonstrates SYNTH execution deterministically without a live agent.

replayBranches.rationale: Allowed interpretation: demo adapter simulates AI execution deterministically.

replayBranches.name: Light-theme default

input.description: Homepage proposal defaulting to a light workspace theme with an optional dark mode toggle.

replayBranches.rationale: Allowed variation within LDS-002 token scale.

lifecycleStages.expectedState: Approved if complete; rejected if insufficient
Blocked unless Divergence Gate is aligned
Reject if outcome diverges from intent

certificationPrinciples: Every drift class must have at least one replay branch.
Every drift-class replay must be intercepted before implementation.
Every valid branch must remain admissible.
Replaying the same branch must produce the same outcome.
